purpose: The O1/O2 graphml coercion arms (slice-5 review observations, never cased until now), observed by colliding LATER FACTS with the coerced attributes under the default static_graph_facts=true: at t=0 only ow='1, 1' leaves a row ([1,1] under the bare key — int() strips the space the isdigit branch rejects, O2), while inv='1,0' (the inverted pair, O1), inv='0,1', pw='0, 1', and nz='-0,1' (int('-0')=0) all leave none. From t=1 the user facts and the re-applied static graph-attribute-facts interleave, and every observation is deterministic and identical cross-engine: the node-side facts record no-change trace rows and the filter shows X1 pinned at the INVERTED [1,0] and X2/X3/X5 at the vacuous [0,1] — no later fact ever sticks on a graph-stated label ([1,1], [0.5,1], and [0.7,1] all fail to land; the static re-application wins every timestep); the edge side resolves differently — both einv user facts bank [1.0,1.0]->[0.0,1.0] resolve rows and the X1->X2 graph fact re-applies as [1,0]->[0,1] — the node/edge asymmetry of the resolve arm. The t=0 silence plus the t>=1 frozen values are the observable form of B23's static pin; the ow/pw/nz trio is B24's whitespace-tolerance asymmetry.
provenance: corpus seed — batch §B23/§B24 (docs/ledger/phase3-adjudication-batch.md) and the slice-5 review's rv-ladder-extra probes (docs/reviews/2026-07-07-phase3-slice5-review.md O1/O2); graphml_parser.py:44-55 + the resolve/static-fact arms at the pin; screened live 2026-07-12 (4 fresh pin processes, digest-stable); fixture harness/fixtures/attr-static-pin.graphml
